会議で「いつも同じ人が話している」「終わってから本音が出てくる」状態は、組織の意: Being in a meeting where the same people are always talking and the truth comes out after the meeting is over significantly reduces the quality of the organization's decision making. Inclusive meeting management is a technology that brings out the insights of all participants. In this article, we will introduce 10 practical techniques.
議題と論点を24時間以上前に共有。即興で考えるのが苦手なメンバーも、準備して参加: Share agendas and talking points more than 24 hours in advance. Even members who are not good at improvisation can prepare and participate.
会議冒頭に資料を読む時間を取るのではなく、事前に各自で読んでくる前提に。会議は「: Instead of taking the time to read the material at the beginning of the meeting, the premise is that you will read it to yourself in advance. The meeting is positioned as a "place to read and discuss materials".
「呼ばれているから」ではなく「貢献できるから」参加する原則。観客は招かない、参加: The principle of participating “because I can contribute” rather than “because I am called”. Spectators are not invited, and participants are responsible for actively contributing.
4. 冒頭の「全員一言」: 4. Say one word to everyone at the beginning
会議の最初の2〜3分、全員が一言話す時間を作る。発言の最初の一歩を集団で踏むこと: During the first two to three minutes of the meeting, everyone has time to talk. Taking the first step of your speech as a group will increase your participation rate later on.
5. 発言の偏りを観察し介入する: 5. Observe and intervene in speech bias
ファシリテーターは発言量を意識的に観察。特定の人が話し続けている、特定の人が一度: The facilitator observes the amount of speech consciously. If a particular person keeps talking, if a particular person has never spoken, explicitly shake it to someone else.
ある意見が出たら、「他の人はどう感じる？」と意図的に追加意見を引き出します。最初: When you hear an opinion, say, “How do other people feel?” to intentionally elicit additional input. The effect of preventing sympathetic pressure on the first opinion.
口頭発言が苦手な人にはチャットで意見を募ります。リモート会議では特に有効。ファシ: For those who are not good at speaking up, we will ask for their opinions in the chat. Especially useful for remote meetings. Facilitator reads the chat out loud and shares it with everyone.
8. 「沈黙の5秒」を活用: 8. Make use of 5 Seconds of Silence
質問の後、すぐに反応がなくても5秒待ちます。考える時間を許容することで、より深い: After the question, wait 5 seconds even if there is no immediate response. Allowing time to think can lead to deeper answers.
会議で決まった事項、宿題、次のステップを文書化し、全員に共有。「言った/言わない: Document and share meeting decisions, homework, and next steps. Prevents “say/don't say”.
10. 会議後の個別フォロー: 10. Follow up individually after the meeting
会議中に発言が少なかったメンバーに、後で個別に「何か追加意見はある？」と聞きます: Members who spoke little during the meeting were later asked individually, "Do you have any additional comments?" ”, indicating that the meeting is not the only opportunity to express an opinion.
1. 発言の途中で遮る: 1. Get in the middle of what you're saying
遮ることは「あなたの意見は重要でない」というメッセージになります。最後まで聞く規: Blocking is a message that your opinion doesn't matter. The discipline of listening to the end.
「それは難しい」「過去にやった」と即座に否定すると、心理的安全性が下がります。「: Instantly denying “it's hard” or “I've done it in the past” reduces psychological safety. Say something like, “Interesting idea, tell me more about it.”
3. 内輪ネタを多用する: 3. Use a lot of inner ring tape
古参メンバーだけが分かる略語や文脈は、新メンバーや外部参加者を排除します。意識的: Abbreviations and contexts known only to veteran members exclude new members and external participants. Consciously add an explanation.
4. 議論を独占する役職者の容認: 4. Acceptance of the position that monopolizes the discussion
役職者が議論を独占している状態を放置すると、他のメンバーの貢献意欲が削がれます。: Letting a position holder monopolize the discussion reduces other members' willingness to contribute. The facilitator is responsible for intervening.
インクルーシブな会議を継続する組織は、意思決定の質が向上し、組織の多様性が活かさ: Organizations that continue to have inclusive meetings will be better able to make better decisions and capitalize on the diversity of their organization. Increasing the number of people who feel they deserve to be in the meeting also makes the meeting itself more efficient.

心理的安全性入門：明日からできる5つの実践: Intro to Psychological Safety: 5 Practices for Tomorrow
「心理的安全性」というキーワードに触れたばかりの方に向けた入門特集。理論の深掘り: An introductory feature for those who have just touched on the keyword "psychological safety". It is structured so that you can learn step by step from the deepening of the theory to the practice in the field and the application to the engineer organization.
心理的安全性は、Google の Project Aristotle 以降、組織: Psychological safety has been a central theme in organizational development since Google's Project Aristotle. However, there are many cases where it is misunderstood as "a friendly workplace" or "avoiding conflict", and the original meaning and effect have not spread widely.
まず誤解の解消（理論編）、次に計測の落とし穴（実装編）、そしてエンジニア組織での: First, the elimination of misunderstandings (theory edition), then the pitfalls of measurement (implementation edition), and specific examples in the engineer organization (application edition), and finally 1: 1 practice (operation edition). Reading in order raises the level of psychological safety from “word” to “implementation”.
心理的安全性をこれから組織に導入したいマネージャー・人事担当者向け。誤解の解消か: For managers and HR personnel who want to introduce psychological safety in their organizations in the future. From clearing up misunderstandings to practicing, measuring, and applying to your engineering organization, here are five articles to guide you along the way. Each article can be read independently, but if you read it in order, you will understand it systematically.

リモート開発組織のドキュメント文化: Document Culture in Remote Development Organizations
リモート開発組織の生産性は、ドキュメント文化の質に大きく依存します。対面なら口頭: The productivity of a remote development organization depends heavily on the quality of the document culture. In-person verbal information sharing must be documented remotely. In this article, we will organize specific measures to root the "writing culture" and failure patterns.
理由1: 非同期コラボの基盤: Reason 1: The foundation of asynchronous collaboration
異なる時間帯で働くメンバーが協働するには、文書化された情報が不可欠。口頭の暗黙知: Documented information is essential for members working in different time zones to work together. Organizations that rely on verbal tacit knowledge are reliably clogged with remoteness.
新メンバーが組織を理解する主な手段がドキュメント。質の高いドキュメントがあれば、: Documentation is the primary way new members understand your organization. Quality documents can take up to three months to catch up in one month.
過去の意思決定の理由が文書として残っていることで、後から「なぜそう決めたか」を検: Because the reasons for past decisions remain in the form of documents, we can later validate why we decided to do so. Organizational learning builds.
書く文化を育てる5つの施策: 5 ways to foster a culture of writing
「会議の前にドキュメントを作る」を原則化。会議は「ドキュメントを読んで議論する場: Based on the principle of "creating a document before the meeting". A meeting is a “place to read and discuss documents” and abolish meetings that discuss from scratch.
施策2: 議事録は必須・全社公開: Measure 2: Minutes are mandatory and company-wide
全ての会議に議事録作成を義務付け、全社で閲覧可能にします。特定の秘匿情報を除き、: Require meeting minutes for all meetings and make them viewable company-wide. Default disclosure policy, except for certain confidential information.
施策3: ADR（Architecture Decision Records）の: Policy 3: Introduction of Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
重要な技術判断は ADR として文書化。「なぜこの選択をしたか」「他の選択肢は何: Important technical decisions are documented as ADRs. Leave "Why did you make this choice?" "What were the other options?"
施策4: 「ドキュメントに無いことは新人が書く」ルール: Policy 4: "What is not in the document is written by the newcomer" rule
新メンバーが質問して暗黙知を引き出したら、その答えを新メンバー自身がドキュメント: When a new member asks a question to elicit tacit knowledge, the new member documents the answer. This ensures that the documentation is continuously enriched.
マネージャーの評価項目に「チームのドキュメント品質」を含めます。これがないと、書: Include “Team Document Quality” in the manager's endorsement. Without it, you won't have time to write.
「完成度の高いドキュメントだけを公開すべき」と考えると、何も書かれなくなります。: If you think you should only publish documents with a high level of completeness, you will not be able to write anything. Even in the WIP (Work in Progress) state, it is necessary to have a culture of publishing.
古いドキュメントが残っていると、新メンバーが混乱します。四半期に1回、ドキュメン: If there are any old documents left, the new members will be confused. Set aside time for document inventory once a quarter.
Notion、Confluence、Wiki、Google Docs、Markd: Mixing Notion, Confluence, Wiki, Google Docs, Markdown files, etc. will dissipate information. The "original is here" is clearly determined.
長期保存・検索性重視: Emphasis on long-term storage and searchability
Notion、Confluence などのナレッジベース。組織の知識資産として蓄: Knowledge bases such as Notion, Confluence, etc. Accumulated as an organization's knowledge asset.
Google Docs、Figma、Miro などのリアルタイム協働ツール。生成: Real-time collaboration tools like Google Docs, Figma, and Miro. As a place for generation and editing.
GitHub の README、Wiki、Discussions。コードと近い場: GitHub READMEs, Wikis, and Discussions. Information to place close to the code.
ドキュメント文化は3〜6ヶ月で根付くものではありません。1年〜2年の継続的な意識: Document culture does not take root in three to six months; it requires a year to two years of continuous awareness and leadership leadership paradigm (management writes a lot). Once rooted, it serves as a long-term competitive advantage for the organization.

1on1の議題が日常業務に集中すると、長期的なキャリア対話の機会が失われます。年: When one-on-one agendas are focused on day-to-day work, long-term career dialogue opportunities are lost. It is too late and too infrequent to talk about your career with just one evaluation interview per year. This article explains the 3 career questions that you should incorporate into 1: 1s and how to use them effectively.
質問1: 「3年後、どんな仕事をしていたいですか？」: Question 1: “What would you like to do in three years?”
長期視点での志向を引き出す質問。具体的な役職ではなく、「どんな種類の問題に取り組: Questions that elicit a long-term perspective. Instead of asking for a specific job title, ask “What kind of problems do you want to work on?” and “What kind of impact do you want to have?”
質問2: 「そのために、今の半年で身につけたいことは何ですか？」: Question 2: “So what do you want to learn in the next six months?”
長期目標を中期のスキル開発に接続する質問。漠然とした願望ではなく、具体的なスキル: Questions that connect long-term goals to medium-term skill development. Unleash specific skills and experiences rather than vague aspirations.
質問3: 「組織として、それをサポートするために何ができますか？」: Question 3: “As an organization, what can I do to support it?”
個人の成長を組織が支える姿勢を示す質問。研修機会の提供、新しい役割の付与、メンタ: Questions that demonstrate an organization's commitment to supporting personal growth. Provide tangible support, such as training opportunities, new roles, and mentoring settings.
初回は浅い回答（「マネージャーになりたい」「専門性を深めたい」）で構いません。3: Begin with a shallow answer (“I want to be a manager” or “I want to deepen my expertise”), and after 3-4 repetitions, you will see an intrinsic preference. Answering the question itself can also encourage your introspection.
「半年で何をすれば3年後に近づけるか」を一緒に考えます。マネージャーが答えを与え: Together, we will think about what we can do in six months to get closer to three years. It is important for managers to materialize in their own language rather than giving answers. When asked, “I want to gain management experience,” I asked, “What experience specifically?" I will dig deeper.
マネージャーが「これができます」と提示するのではなく、本人から要望を引き出します: Instead of your manager saying, “I can do this,” pull the request from you. Get specific requests, such as “I want to be trained,” “I want to be involved in a new project,” or “I want to talk to someone from another department.”
毎回の1on1で3問を扱うと重くなりすぎます。四半期に1回、「キャリア1on1」: If you deal with 3 questions in each 1: 1, it becomes too heavy. Once a quarter, it is realistic to set aside time as "career 1: 1".
キャリア1on1の前に、過去の対話で本人が語っていた志向をメモから振り返ります。: Before your career 1: 1, take notes to reflect on your preferences from past conversations. "I said 100 last time, is there any change?” with continuity.
3問への回答を記録し、半年・1年単位で変化を観察します。志向の変化は、本人の成長: Answers to the three questions will be recorded and changes will be observed on a semi-annual/yearly basis. The change in orientation reflects the person's growth and environmental impact and is worth understanding as an organization.
「君にはマネージャー職が向いていると思う」のような誘導は、本人の真の志向を歪めま: Induction, such as “I think you deserve a managerial position,” distorts the person's true orientation. A manager's job is to bring out their own aspirations and help them achieve them.
キャリア1on1は評価の場ではありません。「今の評価」と「将来の方向性」を分けて: Career 1: 1s are not a place to evaluate. By discussing "current evaluation" and "future direction" separately, a place where the person can speak openly is established.
キャリア対話を定期的に行う組織は、メンバーの定着率と内発的動機が向上します。「組: Organizations that have regular career conversations increase retention and intrinsic motivation. A sense that your organization is taking its future seriously is a motivating factor that goes beyond pay. One of the strongest players in the prevention of turnover.
日常業務の話に偏る1on1。長期的なキャリアの対話を組み込むための「3つの質問」: 1: 1s that are biased towards everyday business. Includes an implementation guide that explains the “three questions” for embedding long-term career dialogue, as well as the purpose of each question and how to listen effectively.
組織の心理的負債を可視化する: Visualize the psychological debt of the organization
「技術的負債」は広く認知されるようになりましたが、組織には「心理的負債」も蓄積し: “Technical debt” is now widely recognized, but the organization also accumulates “psychological debt.” This includes unresolved interpersonal issues, unspoken grievances, and memories of unjust decisions. If left unvisually, it will gradually undermine the performance of the organization. In this article, we will organize concepts and visualization methods.
心理的負債は、組織内で蓄積された「処理されない感情・記憶」の総量です。技術的負債: Psychological debt is the total amount of “unprocessed emotions and memories” accumulated within an organization. If technical debt is "moving but not beautiful code", psychological debt is "moving but not healthy relationships and memories".
心理的負債の3つの源泉: The Three Sources of Psychological Debt
「あの時の対立」「言われた一言」「不公正に感じた評価」などが、当事者の心に残った: "Conflict at that time", "A word said", "Evaluation that felt unfair", etc. are not handled while remaining in the minds of the parties. On the surface, they work together, but there is a big shadow on the build-up of trust.
「言っても変わらない」と諦められた不満が、組織内に潜在化している状態。アンケート: A state in which a dissatisfaction that has been given up "does not change even if I say it" is latent in the organization. Although it does not appear in the questionnaire, it can be observed in a way that the air always cools down in certain topics.
源泉3: 不公正に感じた過去の意思決定: Source 3: Past decisions that felt unfair
過去のレイオフ、評価、昇進判断などへの記憶。実際の判断の妥当性とは別に、「不公正: Memories of past layoffs, ratings, promotions, etc. Apart from the validity of the actual judgment, the perception that it was unfair also affects the trust in the current decision.
四半期に1回、定量的なサーベイを実施。「未解決と感じる問題はありますか？」「組織: Conduct a quarterly quantitative survey. “Do you have any unresolved issues?" "How much do you trust the organization?" "" How frank? "” and other indicators of psychological debt.
外部ファシリテーターを招き、チーム全体で「組織として処理しきれていないこと」を話: A place to bring in external facilitators and discuss “what we can't handle as an organization” with the whole team. About once every six months. Since it is difficult to be honest when the manager is the host, an external perspective is required.
退職者のフィードバックを集約すると、組織が「処理できなかった問題」のパターンが見: Aggregating the feedback of the Leavers reveals a pattern of “issues that couldn't be handled” by the organization. A genuine treasure trove that is difficult for incumbents to come by.
方法4: マネージャー間の率直な情報共有: Method 4: Candid information sharing between managers
マネージャー陣が月1回集まり、各チームの「ホットスポット」を共有します。組織横断: A team of managers meets once a month to share “hotspots” for each team. Across the organization, you may see patterns of repetition.
負債を返済する3つのアプローチ: 3 Ways to Pay Off Your Debt
アプローチ1: 透明な対話の場を設ける: Approach 1: Provide a transparent space for dialogue
未解決の問題について、組織として正面から議論する場を設けます。「過去のあの決定に: Provide a space for the organization to discuss unresolved issues head-on. An attitude such as "Let's explain and talk again about that decision in the past" will lead to the elimination of debt.
アプローチ2: 仕組みの改善で同じ問題を繰り返さない: Approach 2: Improvements don't repeat the same issue
過去の問題の原因が制度や仕組みにある場合、その制度を見直します。「同じ問題は繰り: If past problems are caused by a system or mechanism, review the system. An organization's commitment not to repeat the same issues rebuilds trust.
「過去にこういう問題があったことを認識しています」「申し訳なく思っています」とい: The message “I'm aware of these issues in the past” and “I'm sorry” symbolize the organization's candor. It is important to face the front without hiding it.
心理的負債を定期的に可視化し、返済する組織は、長期的に強い結束力と率直さを維持で: Organizations that regularly visualize and repay psychological debt can maintain strong cohesion and frankness over the long term. Conversely, even if it is working on the surface, the vitality of the tissue will be greatly reduced in 3 to 5 years. Just like technical debt, it is an area that requires regular investment.
技術的負債と同様に、組織にも「心理的負債」が蓄積します。可視化されないまま放置す: Similar to technical debt, there is also a buildup of “psychological debt” in the organization. We will explain the concept of psychological debt that corrupts the organization when left unvisually and the specific methods to visualize it both quantitatively and qualitatively.
可視化されないものは改善できない: What is not visible cannot be improved
プロジェクトレトロスペクティブの形骸化を防ぐ: Prevent Project Retrospective from becoming corrupted
レトロスペクティブ（振り返り）は、スクラム導入企業で広く実施されています。しかし: Retrospectives are widely implemented in Scrum-implementing companies. However, in many organizations, retro has become a ritual of "writing a routine KPT and ending it". In this article, we will organize the design that prevents disintegration and produces essential learning.
「会議が長い」「ドキュメントが少ない」が毎回 Problem 欄に書かれるが、何: "Long meetings" and "few documents" are written in the Problem column every time, but nothing changes. This is a sign that Try has no implementation capability or has not reached the root cause of the problem.
「もっとコミュニケーションを取る」「効率化を意識する」のような抽象 Try は実: No abstract try like “communicate more” or “be more efficient” is performed. Unspecific actions do not change anything as a result.
「特に問題は無い」「うまく行っている」が多数派になる。本当に問題がないのか、心理: "There is no particular problem" "It is going well" becomes the majority. It is necessary to determine whether there is really no problem or whether the psychological safety is low and cannot be said.
工夫1: フォーマットを毎回変える: Idea 1: Change the format every time
KPT、Mad/Sad/Glad、Start/Stop/Continue、Sai: Rotate multiple retro formats such as KPT, Mad/Sad/Glad, Start/Stop/Continue, Sailboat, 4Ls, etc. In the same format, the brain goes into "regular" mode, which reduces deep awareness.
「全部について振り返ろう」だと議論が散ります。「今回はコミュニケーションだけ」「: “Let's look back at it all,” they argue. If you narrow down the themes of "this time only communication" and "this time only quality", you can dig deeper.
Problem を見つけたら、必ず「なぜ？」を5回繰り返します。表層的な対症療法: When you find a problem, be sure to ask “Why?” ”5 times. Dig until you reach the root cause, not a superficial symptomatic treatment.
Try は Specific、Measurable、Achievable、Rel: Try meets the smart criteria of Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant and Time-bound. Specify up to the level of "by next week, X will document X".
毎回の冒頭で、前回 Try の進捗を確認。実行されたか、効果があったかを検証して: At the beginning of each try, check the progress of the previous try. Validate if it was executed or effective and then create a discipline to move on to the new Try.
マネージャーが常にファシリテーターをやると、構造が固定化します。メンバーが交代で: The structure is fixed when the manager is always the facilitator. Various progression styles are created when members take turns as facilitators.
議論が止まったとき、すぐに発言を促すのではなく、5〜10秒の沈黙を許容します。考: When the discussion stops, allow 5-10 seconds of silence instead of prompting immediately. Time to think creates deep awareness.
「〇〇さんが〇〇しないから」のような個人攻撃には即座に介入し、「仕組みの問題とし: Immediately intervene in a personal attack such as "because" and reframe it as a problem of how it works.
四半期に1回、「レトロのレトロ」を実施します。レトロの進め方そのものを振り返り、: Once a quarter, we will carry out "Retro Retro". A place to reflect on and improve the retro process itself. Without it, the retro will become obsolete.
機能するレトロを持つチームは、学習速度が継続的に向上し、組織の改善文化が定着しま: Teams with functional retrospectives will continue to improve their learning speed and anchor a culture of organizational improvement. “Reflective organizations” gain a competitive advantage in the long run.
スプリント末のレトロスペクティブが「お決まりのKPT」で終わっていませんか？形骸: Does the retrospective at the end of the sprint end with the usual KPT? With practical techniques, we will explain retro design that prevents debris and produces essential learning.
北欧型 work-life integration の本質: The essence of Nordic work-life integration
「ワークライフバランス」は仕事と生活を別物として「均衡」させる発想ですが、北欧諸: "Work-life balance" is an idea to "balance" work and life as a separate thing, but in the Nordic countries, the idea of "work-life integration" is the mainstream. The two are not separate, but designed to enrich each other. This article organizes the essence and implementation.
仕事の時間 vs 生活の時間を競合関係と捉え、両者を「適切な比率」で配分する考え: The idea of viewing work time versus life time as a competitive relationship and allocating the two in an "appropriate ratio". The premise is that if you work more, your life will decrease.
仕事と生活を独立した変数として捉えるのではなく、相互作用する2つの要素として設計: The idea of designing work and life as two interacting elements rather than as separate variables. Assume a complementary relationship where quality of work enhances quality of life and quality of life supports creativity at work.
北欧型統合を支える4要素: The Four Elements of Nordic Integration
始業・終業時間が個人の生活リズムに合わせて変動可能。子どもの送迎・通院・趣味の練: Opening and closing times can fluctuate according to the rhythm of an individual's life. The flexibility to incorporate child transportation, hospital visits, hobby practice, etc. during business hours is institutionalized.
完全リモート、ハイブリッド、出社中心など、業務特性と個人の状況に応じて選べる。場: You can choose according to your business characteristics and personal situation, such as completely remote, hybrid, and work center. The premise is that fixing the location is not essential to the outcome.
育児期の時短勤務、サバティカル休暇（数ヶ月の長期休暇）、フル稼働期の選択など、ラ: Workload can be adjusted according to the life stage, such as working shorter hours during childcare, sabbatical leave (a few months of leave), and choosing a full working period.
稼働量が変動しても評価制度が公正に機能する設計。時間ではなく成果と貢献を評価軸に: Designed to function fairly even when workload fluctuates. By using achievements and contributions as the axis of evaluation rather than time, the above three types of flexibility work.
コアタイムの撤廃、フレックスタイム制の導入から始める。最も導入ハードルが低く、効: Starting with the abolition of core time and the introduction of the flex-time system. The introduction hurdle is the lowest, and the effect is felt quickly.
週何日リモート可能か、業務特性別の方針を文書化。出社必須日を最小化する設計。: How many days of the week can you be remote, and document your business-specific policies. Designed to minimize mandatory days at work.
育児・介護・学び直しに対応した時短勤務制度、サバティカル制度の検討。これは制度設: Consideration of a time-short work system and a sabbatical system corresponding to childcare, nursing care, and re-learning. This requires not only system design, but also linkage with the evaluation system.
時間ベースの評価指標を排除し、成果ベースに移行。同時に「成果」の定義を明確化（短: Eliminate time-based metrics and move to outcome-based. At the same time, clarify the definition of "results" (short-term results vs. long-term contributions).
これらの変化は、制度を整えても文化として定着するまでに3〜5年かかります。経営層: These changes will take three to five years to establish themselves as a culture, even if they are institutionalized. Continuous communication from management and leadership paradigms (management itself practicing flexible work styles) determine the speed at which it will take hold.
従業員エンゲージメントの向上、優秀人材の獲得力強化。: Increased employee engagement and talent acquisition.
離職率の低下、女性管理職比率の向上。: Decrease in turnover and increase in the proportion of female managers.
組織の創造性向上、イノベーション創出力の強化、社会的評価の向上。: Improve the creativity of the organization, strengthen the ability to create innovation, and improve social evaluation.
「柔軟性」を「全てが自由」と誤解すると、混乱が生じます。柔軟性は「明確なルールと: Confusion arises when “flexibility” is misinterpreted as “everything is free.” Flexibility is “freedom based on clear rules and mutual trust.” Freedom without rules is irresponsible.
「ワークライフバランス」を超えて「work-life integration（統: A Nordic way of working that goes beyond "work-life balance" to achieve "work-life integration". The essence and implementation method in the Japanese context will be explained from both the cultural background and the organizational system.
統合は相互信頼と明確なルールの上に: Integration is based on mutual trust and clear rules
リモート時代のチームビルディング 12の実験: Team Building 12 Experiments in the Remote Era
「リモートでチームビルディング」と聞くと、多くの人が「画面越しの飲み会」を想像し: When you hear “team building remotely”, many people imagine a “cross-screen drinking party”, but this is not effective. There are many team building techniques that have been developed with remote assumptions. In this article, we present 12 validated experiments by effect.
各メンバーが自分の人生の重要な出来事を時系列で共有。30分程度。お互いの背景理解: Each member shares the important events of their life in chronological order. About 30 minutes. The understanding of each other's backgrounds is deepened at once.
1on1の時間、双方が散歩しながら音声通話。画面を見ない対話で、リラックスした会: One-on-one time, voice calls while both parties take a walk. Screen-free conversations lead to relaxed conversations.
月1回、メンバーが自分の趣味を10分でプレゼン。仕事外の側面を共有することで、人: Once a month, members present their hobbies in 10 minutes. Sharing aspects outside of work increases your sense of three-dimensionality as a person.
カテゴリ2: 共同体験を作る実験: Category 2: Experiments to create a shared experience
有料サービスを利用したチーム参加型脱出ゲーム。協力プレイで自然な役割分担が発生し: Team-based escape game with paid services. The natural division of roles occurs in cooperative play, and the strengths that are usually invisible can be seen.
同じ書籍を読み、月1回ディスカッション。思考の癖や価値観が共有される。書籍は組織: Read the same books and discuss once a month. Thinking habits and values are shared. Recommended books related to organizational issues.
同じレシピを各自が同時にオンラインで作る。失敗も含めた共有体験が思い出になる。: Each person makes the same recipe online at the same time. Shared experiences, including failures, are memorable.
業務タスクを2人で同時に進める時間。営業・デザイン・分析など職種を問わず応用可能: Time for two people to work on a task at the same time. It can be applied regardless of job type, such as sales, design, and analysis. Knowledge transfer accelerates.
月1回、各自が「最近やらかしたこと」を共有する場。心理的安全性の土壌が育つ。: Once a month, a place where each person shares what they have recently done. The soil of psychological safety is nurtured.
